zh.lifeSummary: 當Harrison Riley Massey於1860年4月8日出生在美國喬治亞哈特哈特韋爾時，他的父親James P. Massey37歲，母親Frances Townsend36歲。他於1883年8月26日與Lura Ann Bray在美國喬治亞哈特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他於1900年住在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林安賀且於1910年住在美國阿肯色州加蘭安賀 堂史普。他去世於1913年6月4日在美國阿肯色州加蘭霍特 斯普林斯享年53歲，埋葬在美國阿肯色州加蘭霍特 斯普林斯福 盧普 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Harrison Riley Massey于1860年4月8日出生在美国乔治亚州哈特哈特韋爾时，他的父亲James P. Massey37岁，母亲Frances Townsend36岁。他于1883年8月26日与Lura Ann Bray在美国乔治亚州哈特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他于1900年住在美国阿肯色州霍特 斯普林安賀且于1910年住在美国阿肯色州加蘭安賀 堂史普。他去世于1913年6月4日在美国阿肯色州加蘭霍特 斯普林斯享年53岁，埋葬在美国阿肯色州加蘭霍特 斯普林斯福 盧普 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: Harrison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which originated as a patronymic meaning ‘son of Harry’. Use as a given name may have been influenced by the U.S. presidents  William Henry Harrison  (  1773–1841 ) and his grandson  Benjamin Harrison  (  1833–1901 ). A more recent influence is the actor  Harrison Ford  (  b. 1942 ).

nameMeanings.name: Riley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: In some cases a transferred use of the English surname, a local name from a place named with Old English ryge ‘rye’ + lēah ‘wood, clearing’. There is one such place in Devon and another in Lancashire. In other cases it probably represents a respelling of the Irish surname Reilly, which is from an old Irish personal name, Raghallach, of unknown origin. While it is presently more popular for boys than girls in Britain, the converse is true in the United States.

variants.name: Ryley
meanings: Respelling of   Riley .

historicEvents.description: Civil War History - Some 11,000 Georgians gave their lives in defense of their state a state that suffered immense destruction. But wars end brought about an even more dramatic figure to tell: 460,000 African-Americans were set free from the shackles of slavery to begin new lives as free people.
Arkansas supplied an estimated 50,000 men to the Confederate Army andabout 15,000 to the Union Army.

historicEvents.title: A Treaty with Hawaii
historicEvents.description: In the Mid 1870s, The United States sought out the Kingdom of Hawaii to make a free trade agreement. The Treaty gave the Hawaiians access to the United States agricultural markets and it gave the United States a part of land which later became Pearl Harbor.

historicEvents.title: A New Civil Rights Act
historicEvents.description: During the response to civil rights violations to African Americans, the bill was passed giving African Americans equal treatment in public accommodations, public transportation, and to prohibit exclusion from jury duty. While many in the public opposed this law, the African Americans greatly favored it.

historicEvents.title: The Sherman Antitrust Act
historicEvents.description: This Act tried to prevent the raising of prices by restricting trade. The purpose of the Act was to preserve a competitive marketplace to protect consumers from abuse.

historicEvents.title: The Last Public Hanging in Georgia
historicEvents.description: The last public hanging in Georgia was on September 28, 1893. The General Assembly prohibited public executions in December 1893. Prior to this law, Georgians commonly traveled to witness scheduled public executions.

historicEvents.title: Department of Commerce and Labor
historicEvents.description: A short-lived Cabinet department which was concerned with controlling the excesses of big business. Later being split and the Secretary of Commerce and Labor splitting into two separate positions.

historicEvents.title: The Atlanta Race Riot
historicEvents.description: The Atlanta Race Riot of 1906 occurred on the evening of September 22 through September 24. A newspaper reported the rapes of four white women by African American men. Fueled by pre-existing racial tensions, these reports enraged white men who then arranged gangs to attack African American men. Over the next few days, several thousand white men joined in and in the end, 26 people were killed and many were injured.

en.lifeSummary: When Harrison Riley Massey was born on 8 April 1860, in Hartwell, Hart, Georgia, United States, his father, James P. Massey, was 37 and his mother, Frances Townsend, was 36. He married Lura Ann Bray on 26 August 1883, in Hart, Georgia, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. He lived in Antioch, Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States in 1900 and Antioch Township, Garland, Arkansas, United States in 1910. He died on 4 June 1913, in Hot Springs, Garland, Arkansas, United States, at the age of 53, and was buried in Fourche Loupe Cemetery, Hot Springs, Garland, Arkansas, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Harrison Riley Massey est né le 8 avril 1860, à Hartwell, Hart, Géorgie, États-Unis, son père, James P. Massey, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Frances Townsend, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Lura Ann Bray le 26 août 1883, à Hart, Géorgie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Il a vécu à Antioch, Hot Spring, Arkansas, États-Unis en 1900 et à Antioch Township, Garland, Arkansas, États-Unis en 1910. Il est décédé le 4 juin 1913, à Hot Springs, Garland, Arkansas, États-Unis, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Fourche Loupe Cemetery, Hot Springs, Garland, Arkansas, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Harrison Riley Massey am 8. April 1860 in Hartwell, Hart, Georgia, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater James P. Massey 37 und seine Mutter Frances Townsend 36. Er hat am 26. August 1883 in Hart, Georgia, Vereinigte Staaten Lura Ann Bray geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1900 hat er in Antioch, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten und 1910 in Antioch Township, Garland, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 4. Juni 1913 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Hot Springs, Garland, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Fourche Loupe Cemetery, Hot Springs, Garland, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Harrison Riley Massey è nato il il 8 aprile 1860 nel Hartwell, Hart, Georgia, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, James P. Massey, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Frances Townsend, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Lura Ann Bray il 26 agosto 1883, nel Hart, Georgia, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie. Vissuto a Antioch, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1900 e nel Antioch Township, Garland, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1910. Morì il 4 giugno 1913, nel Hot Springs, Garland, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolto a Fourche Loupe Cemetery, Hot Springs, Garland, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Harrison Riley Masseyが1860年4月8日にアメリカ合衆国ジョージア州ハートハートウエルで生まれた時，父James P. Masseyは37歳，母Frances Townsendは36歳であった。1883年8月26日にアメリカ合衆国ジョージア州ハートでLura Ann Brayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1900年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ホート スプリングアンティオチに，1910年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ガーランドアンティオチ トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼は1913年6月4日にアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ガーランドホート スプリングズで53歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ガーランドホート スプリングズフォウルチェ ロウペ セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Harrison Riley Massey(이)가 1860년 4월 8일 미국 조지아 주 하트 하트웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James P. Massey(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Frances Townsend(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1883년 8월 26일 미국 조지아 주 하트에서 Lura Ann Bray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1900년도에 미국 아칸소 주 호트 스프링 앤티오치에서, 1910년도에 미국 아칸소 주 가랜드 앤티오치 타운시프에서 살았다. 그는 1913년 6월 4일 미국 아칸소 주 가랜드 호트 스프링스에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 아칸소 주 가랜드 호트 스프링스 포우르체 루프 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Harrison Riley Massey nasceu em 8 de abril de 1860, em Hartwell, Hart, Geórgia, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, James P. Massey, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Frances Townsend, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Lura Ann Bray em 26 de agosto de 1883, em Hart, Geórgia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele viveu em Antioch, Hot Spring, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1900 e em Antioch Township, Garland, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1910. Ele faleceu em 4 de junho de 1913, em Hot Springs, Garland, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Fourche Loupe Cemetery, Hot Springs, Garland, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Harrison Riley Massey родился 8 апреля 1860 г. в местности хартвелл, харт, Джорджия, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени James P. Massey было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Frances Townsend, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Lura Ann Bray 26 августа 1883 г. в местности харт, Джорджия, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он жил в местности антиоч, хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1900 году и в местности антиоч тоуншип, гарланд, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1910 году. Он умер 4 июня 1913 г. в местности хот спрингс, гарланд, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности фоурч лоуп кеметири, хот спрингс, гарланд, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты.
